sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Champion Homes Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results
feed.summary: Champion Homes, Inc. (NYSE: SKY) reported strong first-quarter fiscal 2025 results, with net sales increasing 35.1% to $627.8 million and U.S. homes sold up 35.7%. The company also announced its legal name change to Champion Homes, Inc., aligning with its flagship brand and strategic focus on home attainability. Despite a slight decrease in net income, Adjusted EBITDA grew by 12.2% to $75.0 million.

feed.title: |TrueBlue Appoints Two New Independent Directors
feed.summary: |TrueBlue, Inc. announced the appointment of William Greenblatt and William Seward as independent directors to its Board, effective January 5, 2026. These appointments followed an extensive search and shareholder input, aiming to strengthen operational oversight and accelerate growth. Both new directors bring extensive experience in their respective fields, with Mr. Greenblatt being a pioneer in background screening and Mr. Seward a seasoned global executive in logistics and supply chain.

feed.title: Royal Gold stock hits all-time high at 209.44 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Royal Gold Inc. (NASDAQ: RGLD) stock has reached an all-time high of $209.44, buoyed by a 38.54% 1-year return and a 55.75% year-to-date gain, reflecting strong investor confidence. The company reported record Q3 2025 revenue of $252 million and increased its annual dividend by 6% to $1.90 per share, while analysts like Raymond James maintain an Outperform rating with a $264 price target. This performance highlights Royal Gold's robust business model with an 87.7% gross profit margin and 28.17% revenue growth.

feed.title: Old Market Capital Corp Announces $7 Million Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: Old Market Capital Corp (OMCC) announced a $7 million share repurchase program on December 10, 2025. This program, without a set expiration date, allows for the repurchase of shares based on market conditions, and can be modified or terminated at any time.

feed.title: |‘We have not seen this rosy picture’: ADP’s chief economist warns the real economy is pretty different from Wall Street’s bullish outlook
feed.summary: |This article suggests that the real economic situation is not as positive as Wall Street's optimistic forecasts. ADP's chief economist indicates that their data does not support the widely held bullish outlook. The discrepancy highlights a potential disconnect between financial market sentiment and on-the-ground economic realities.

feed.title: SLB, Shell to Accelerate Digital Solutions for Upstream Operations
feed.summary: SLB and Shell have signed a strategic collaboration agreement to develop digital and AI solutions for accelerating performance and efficiency in upstream operations. This builds on a long-standing relationship, aiming to create agentic AI-powered solutions and an open data and AI infrastructure using SLB’s Lumi platform. The initiative will also inform Shell's digital strategy and contribute to industry-wide digital solution development.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC, guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co., is offering Structured Investments Digital Barrier Notes linked to the Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000, and S&P 500 indices. These notes offer a fixed return of at least 9.50% at maturity if the least performing index is above 70.00% of its initial value, but investors risk losing principal if any index falls below this barrier. The notes are unsecured obligations and come with significant risks, including exposure to the creditworthiness of both JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co.

feed.title: FCX INVESTOR ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a securities class action lawsuit against Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE: FCX) on behalf of investors who suffered losses between February 15, 2022, and September 24, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Freeport-McMoRan made false statements and failed to disclose inadequate safety measures at its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia, leading to heightened risks for workers. Investors have until January 12, 2026, to apply to be lead plaintiff.

feed.title: SnapLogic Named a Visionary in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Data Integration Tools for Seventh Year
feed.summary: SnapLogic has been recognized as a Visionary in Gartner's 2025 Magic Quadrant for Data Integration Tools for the seventh consecutive year, and for the eleventh time in the iPaaS Magic Quadrant. The company was evaluated based on its Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute, highlighting its innovation in AI, no-code approach, and commitment to agentic integration. SnapLogic's platform unifies AI, data, applications, and microservices to empower users in automating workflows and creating intelligent enterprise agents without coding.

feed.title: SnapLogic Named a Visionary in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Data Integration Tools for Seventh Year
feed.summary: SnapLogic has been recognized as a Visionary in Gartner's 2025 Magic Quadrant for Data Integration Tools for the seventh consecutive year, lauded for its Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute. The company's ongoing innovation in AI, intuitive no-code approach, and commitment to agentic integration were key factors, highlighted by advancements like AgentCreator 3.0, SnapGPT updates, and the new SnapLogic Intelligent Modernizer (SLIM) service. This recognition validates SnapLogic's leadership in providing accessible integration capabilities that drive results for enterprises by unifying AI, data, and applications.

feed.title: SO Stock Declines 6% in Past 6 Months: Here's How to Play
feed.summary: The Southern Company's (SO) shares have declined by 5.9% over the past six months, underperforming the utility sector. Despite this, the article outlines several factors that suggest strong investment prospects, including significant large-load growth, a supportive regulatory environment, and a massive capital investment pipeline. The company also boasts high-quality customer growth and a strong dividend history, although its stock trades at a premium valuation compared to its industry peers.

feed.title: BNY Joins U.S. Government's Investment Initiative for Children, Expanding Access to Savings and Financial Opportunity for Employees
feed.summary: BNY announced its participation in a U.S. government initiative to expand financial access for employees' children, becoming one of the first financial services companies to join. The company will match the federal government's $1,000 contribution for eligible newborns of its U.S. employees, doubling the initial investment in their children's savings accounts. This initiative builds on BNY's broader efforts to enhance employees' financial well-being, including equity grants, student loan matching, and improved 401(k) features.

feed.title: 2026 Comeback Picks: 3 S&P Laggards Poised to Break Out
feed.summary: This article identifies Fiserv (FISV), The Trade Desk (TTD), and Deckers Outdoor (DECK) as S&P 500 laggards from 2025 that are poised for a significant rebound in 2026. The author highlights historical patterns of underperforming stocks outperforming the following year, coupled with favorable macroeconomic conditions like anticipated rate cuts and election-related ad spending. Each stock is analyzed with their current performance, market factors, and analyst projections for potential upside.

feed.title: Perma-Fix Environmental Services board rescinds stock option plan amendment and updates bylaws
feed.summary: Perma-Fix Environmental Services' board rescinded a previous amendment to its 2017 Stock Option Plan and updated company bylaws, following a legal challenge regarding vote counting. A new amendment to increase authorized shares by 600,000 is now subject to future stockholder approval. These actions address governance uncertainties and align with Delaware law, occurring as the company works towards profitability despite recent positive Q3 2025 earnings and a new PFAS-free firefighting solution partnership.

feed.title: Global Payments Details the Promise And Perils of Agentic Commerce
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc.'s 2026 Commerce and Payments Trends Report highlights the rise of agentic commerce, where AI agents shop and purchase on behalf of consumers. While it promises increased conversion rates and new revenue, businesses and consumers express reservations primarily concerning security, fraud, and dispute resolution. The report suggests that familiarity and experience will be key to overcoming these qualms, as the technology offers significant benefits through enhanced shopper data for merchants.

feed.title: Baxter Presents Real-World Data at American Society of Health System Pharmacists Meeting Demonstrating Smart Infusion Pump Electronic Medical Record (EMR) Integration May Improve Patient Safety and Nursing Bedside Productivity
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. announced real-world data showing that smart infusion pump integration with hospital EMRs positively impacts patient safety, clinician productivity, and programming compliance. Data from over one million infusions using Baxter's Spectrum IQ pumps at The University of Texas Medical Branch indicated a significant reduction in patient safety alerts and decreased time spent on programming and alert resolution. These findings highlight the potential of connected infusion therapy to enhance patient safety and alleviate clinician workload.

feed.title: T-Mobile Arena (NYSE: MGM) taps Zoox to add robotaxi rides for fans by 2026 events
feed.summary: T-Mobile Arena and Zoox have partnered to introduce autonomous ride-hailing services for event-goers starting in early 2026, aiming to enhance transportation convenience and fan experience. This multi-year agreement makes Zoox an Official Venue Partner, integrating robotaxis with dedicated pickup/drop-off lanes and branding throughout the arena. The collaboration expands Zoox's presence in Las Vegas and builds upon its existing partnership with the Vegas Golden Knights, bringing cutting-edge mobility to millions of visitors annually.

feed.title: Bank Midwest selects Raymond James to support investment program By Investing.com
feed.summary: Bank Midwest has partnered with Raymond James Financial Services' Financial Institutions Division to enhance its wealth management offerings. The rebranded "Midwest Wealth Group" will utilize Raymond James' resources to serve clients across Iowa, Minnesota, and South Dakota, overseeing approximately $692 million in client assets. This move aims to expand and improve wealth management services for Bank Midwest customers.

feed.title: Live videos may be the biggest factor in an eBay stock 2026 rally
feed.summary: The article suggests that live videos could be a significant driver for a potential rally in eBay stock by 2026. This indicates a focus on emerging interactive e-commerce trends and their impact on the company's future performance.

feed.title: CVS Health offering measles vaccine to South Carolina residents amid the state's current outbreak
feed.summary: CVS Health is offering the measles-mumps-rubella (MMR) vaccine at its CVS Pharmacy and MinuteClinic locations in South Carolina due to a current measles outbreak in the state. The vaccine is available to residents aged three and older at CVS Pharmacy, while MinuteClinic provides the second vaccination dose to children aged 4-6. This initiative aims to help protect the public from the highly contagious disease, with both walk-ins and scheduled appointments encouraged.

feed.title: JPMorgan (NYSE: AMJB) issues buffered review notes tied to Dow, Russell 2000, S&P 500
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering structured "Review Notes" (NYSE: AMJB) linked to the performance of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Russell 2000 Index, and S&P 500 Index, maturing in December 2028. These notes feature potential automatic calls with premiums starting in December 2026, offering investors a capped return or downside protection with a 20% buffer if not called. However, investors face the risk of losing up to 80% of their principal if the worst-performing index declines by more than 20% and the notes are not called early.

feed.title: Williams Companies (NYSE:WMB) SVP Terrance Lane Wilson Sells 2,000 Shares
feed.summary: Terrance Lane Wilson, SVP at Williams Companies, sold 2,000 shares of WMB on December 9th at $61.90 per share, reducing his position by 0.67%. The company recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share and reported Q3 EPS of $0.49, missing analyst estimates. Several institutional investors have adjusted their holdings in Williams Companies, and analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $68.21.

feed.title: FedEx (FDX) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: FedEx (FDX) is expected to show flat earnings year-over-year but higher revenues for the quarter ended November 2025. With a Zacks Earnings ESP of +1.38% and a Zacks Rank #2, the company is highly likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate. Investors are advised to consider the company's Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank alongside other factors when making investment decisions.

feed.title: Blackstone lands $3.15B refinancing from Wells Fargo for Stuy Town
feed.summary: Blackstone has secured a $3.15 billion refinancing loan from Wells Fargo for its Stuyvesant Town/Peter Cooper Village property in Manhattan, which it acquired in 2015 for $5.5 billion. This refinancing addresses the upcoming maturity of an existing $3 billion loan and reaffirms Blackstone's commitment to the complex, where it has invested $460 million in renovations and upgrades. The property, known for its 11,000 rent-stabilized units, has also been involved in a recent legal battle over rent protections, which a judge sided with tenants on in 2023.

feed.title: TD Cowen raises Globe Life stock price target to $199 on growth outlook
feed.summary: TD Cowen has increased its price target for Globe Life (NYSE:GL) to $199, maintaining a Buy rating, due to the insurance company's "attractive, scalable business" and strong growth outlook. The firm included Globe Life in its "Best Ideas 2026" list, highlighting its consistent growth, low underwriting risk, and large addressable market in the underserved lower-middle and middle-income U.S. households. Despite trading at a discount compared to historical averages, the company boasts a perfect Piotroski Score of 9 and has paid dividends for 55 consecutive years, suggesting undervaluation relative to its growth prospects.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): Navigating the AI Frontier Amidst Regulatory Headwinds
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) remains a dominant force in the tech industry, driven by its aggressive leadership in AI, robust growth in Google Cloud, and the enduring strength of its core advertising businesses. The company faces significant regulatory challenges, particularly antitrust lawsuits in the U.S. and EU, but its strategic investments in AI infrastructure and diverse portfolio position it for continued long-term growth. Investors are largely bullish, though they remain cautious about ongoing capital expenditures and intense competition in the AI space.

feed.title: PNC’s Demchak bemoans M&A speculation
feed.summary: PNC CEO Bill Demchak expressed skepticism about large bank M&A activity in the near future due to high execution risks and inflated price tags for small-bank sellers. Despite his recent acquisition of FirstBank and plans for organic growth, Demchak criticized the market's excessive focus on M&A speculation over franchise quality. He also defended PNC's acquisition strategy against critics focused solely on metrics like tangible book value dilution.

feed.title: 1-Year High Following Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Expedia Group (NASDAQ:EXPE) has reached a new 52-week high after several analysts, including Jefferies Financial Group, raised their price targets for the stock. The company also reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations for both EPS and revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend. Insider selling activity was noted, while institutional investors have largely increased their positions in the online travel giant.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial appoints Liane Pelletier to board of directors
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial has appointed Liane J. Pelletier to its board of directors, effective Wednesday. Pelletier brings extensive experience in corporate strategy, technology, and governance from her previous roles at Alaska Communications Systems Group and Sprint Corporation, as well as current directorships at Expeditors International of Washington, Inc. and Frontdoor, Inc. The appointment comes as Ameriprise shows strong financial health, with management actively buying back shares and maintaining consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: Oracle’s Larry Ellison Thinks He’s Identified the Next Big AI Business
feed.summary: Oracle CEO Larry Ellison believes the next major AI business will focus on enabling AI models to perform multi-step reasoning on private enterprise data without compromising security. This approach, he argues, will be even more valuable than training AI models on public data, as Oracle databases hold a significant portion of the world's high-value private data. The company is aggressively investing in data centers and hardware, with capital expenditures projected to reach $50 billion, to secure large AI deals in this emerging market.

feed.title: Expeditors International Soars to All-Time Stock High Amidst Robust Performance and Strategic Acumen
feed.summary: Expeditors International of Washington Inc. (NASDAQ: EXPD) reached an unprecedented all-time high stock price of $151.70 USD on December 11, 2025, reflecting strong investor confidence and strategic acumen in navigating global supply chains. This performance highlights the company's asset-light model, technological investments in AI, and customer-centric approach, solidifying its position as a market leader. While facing short-term headwinds, Expeditors is poised for continued growth through e-commerce expansion, supply chain resilience solutions, and sustained investment in technology.

feed.title: SLB Adding Manufacturing Space to Louisiana Technology Center
feed.summary: SLB announced a $30 million expansion plan for its technology center in Shreveport, Louisiana, to increase manufacturing space for digital infrastructure and data center equipment. This project, which transforms a former GM assembly plant, is expected to create 600 new jobs directly and an additional 744 indirect jobs, doubling the facility's footprint. The expansion is supported by a Louisiana incentives package including a $6 million grant and workforce development solutions.

feed.title: Associated Banc-Corp stock hits 52-week high at 27.46 USD
feed.summary: Associated Banc-Corp (ASB) stock reached a new 52-week high of $27.46, closing at $27.48, reflecting year-to-date returns of 17.53%. The bank maintains a 3.55% dividend yield and has sustained dividend payments for 51 consecutive years. Recent strategic initiatives, positive Q3 2025 earnings where they surpassed EPS expectations, and an increased quarterly dividend to $0.24, suggest continued growth and financial stability, despite InvestingPro analysis indicating the stock is slightly overvalued.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation (NMM.DE) Sees Significant Rise on Germany’s XETRA Exchange
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NMM.DE) experienced a significant 5.43% price increase on Germany's XETRA exchange, closing at €83.5 and positioning itself as a top gainer. This rise is attributed to strong technical indicators, a robust market capitalization of €87.748 billion, and positive sentiment within the gold industry, despite global economic challenges. Meyka AI projects a three-year forecast of €185.19 for Newmont, highlighting its strong financial performance and sector resilience.

feed.title: Top Wealth Group (NASDAQ: TWG) closes $5.04M offer, potential $10.08M from warrants
feed.summary: Top Wealth Group Holding (NASDAQ: TWG) announced the closing of a best-efforts public offering of 720,000 units at $7.00 per unit, generating approximately $5.04 million in gross proceeds. Each unit includes one Class A ordinary share and two warrants, potentially raising an additional $10.08 million if all warrants are exercised for cash. The net proceeds are earmarked for general corporate and working capital purposes.

feed.title: Strong winds leave millions in Sao Paulo without power, cutting water and flights
feed.summary: An extratropical cyclone swept through Sao Paulo, leaving about 1.5 million homes and businesses without electricity and damaging infrastructure. The storm caused significant disruptions to power, water supply, and flights, drawing public scrutiny to utility firm Enel. Airport operations were severely affected, with numerous cancellations at both Congonhas and Guarulhos International Airports.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher upgraded at Citi on boost in pharma spending
feed.summary: Citi has upgraded Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to a "buy" rating from "neutral." The upgrade is based on the expectation that Thermo Fisher will benefit from increased R&D spending by pharmaceutical companies. This anticipated boost in pharma spending is attributed to new clarity on drug pricing following recent industry deals.

feed.title: SMCI Declines 9% in a Year: Should You Hold or Fold the Stock?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) shares have decreased by 8.9% in the past year, underperforming the industry. The company faces operational challenges like declining revenues, shrinking margins due to customer concentration, and negative free cash flow from rapid expansion to meet AI demand. Despite competitive pressures, SMCI's high-performance servers, strategic partnerships, and new product families are expected to drive future growth, and the stock currently trades at a discounted valuation, leading to a "Hold" recommendation.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Shares Extend Rally On $2B Buyback
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean (RCL) shares are rallying due to a new $2 billion share repurchase program and supportive macroeconomic tailwinds, including a recent Federal Reserve interest rate cut. This move follows a previous $1 billion buyback, showcasing the company's strong financial health and investment-grade balance sheet. The rate cut is expected to lower borrowing costs for the industry and boost consumer spending, further benefiting the cruise operator.

feed.title: BREAKING: Rezolute, Inc. Shares Down Over 85%; Securities Fraud Investigation by Block & Leviton Could Allow Investors to Recover Losses
feed.summary: Shares of Rezolute, Inc. (RZLT) dropped over 85% after its Phase 3 sunRIZE study failed to meet primary or key secondary endpoints. Block & Leviton is investigating potential securities law violations and encourages investors who lost money to contact them. The firm is considering filing a class action lawsuit to recover losses on behalf of investors.

feed.title: Stifel Nicolaus Maintains 'Hold' on Hershey Amidst Cost Headwinds, Adjusts Price Target
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus has reaffirmed its "Hold" rating on The Hershey Company (NYSE: HSY), adjusting its price target multiple times from $185 to $195 and back to $185. This neutral outlook reflects concerns over elevated cocoa costs and increased tariff expenses, which are expected to impact Hershey's profitability despite strong third-quarter performance. The consistent "Hold" rating suggests that while Hershey is fundamentally strong, external cost pressures are creating uncertainty for its short-term stock trajectory, advising investors to maintain current positions.

feed.title: FedEx (FDX/NYSE): Navigating a Transformative Era in Global Logistics
feed.summary: FedEx (FDX/NYSE) is undergoing a significant transformation with its "DRIVE" program and "Network 2.0" initiatives focused on cost reduction and operational efficiencies. The company, a global leader in logistics, is also planning to spin off FedEx Freight into a separate publicly traded entity by June 2026 to unlock shareholder value. Despite a mixed stock performance in the short term, FedEx shows long-term growth potential and is adapting to industry trends through technology investments and sustainability goals, while facing fierce competition and macroeconomic uncertainties.

feed.title: Industry Veteran Takes Helm as President of Norwegian Cruise Line
feed.summary: Marc Kazlauskas has been appointed as the new president of Norwegian Cruise Line, succeeding David Herrera. Kazlauskas, an industry veteran, joins the company as it prepares for fleet expansion with the Norwegian Luna and significant upgrades to its private island, Great Stirrup Cay. Parent company NCLH CEO Harry Sommer praised Kazlauskas' leadership and commercial expertise, anticipating continued success for the brand.

feed.title: Arista Scales Up Campus Wireless With VESPA Tech: Stock to Aid?
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) has introduced Virtual Ethernet Segment with Proxy ARP (VESPA) technology to enhance large-scale Wi-Fi networks in educational and corporate campuses. This new solution allows seamless roaming for over 500,000 devices and 30,000 wireless access points, utilizing data-center technologies for massive scalability. Coupled with an upgrade to its Autonomous Virtual Assist (AVA) using AI and LLMs for network operations, Arista aims to provide robust, cloud-driven wireless mobility and AI-powered network solutions, strengthening its market competitiveness against rivals like Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Cisco Systems.

feed.title: ADP (Nasdaq: ADP) embeds Thatch ICHRA in RUN to widen flexible health benefits for 900,000 small businesses
feed.summary: ADP announced an embedded integration of Thatch's ICHRA platform into RUN Powered by ADP, targeting over 900,000 small businesses. This integration allows employers to discover, quote, activate, and enroll in ICHRA plans directly within RUN, automating payroll deductions and carrier payments. The move simplifies healthcare access for small businesses and builds on ADP Ventures' previous strategic investment in Thatch.

feed.title: Equinix targets 150MW campus in Lelystad, Netherlands
feed.summary: Equinix is reportedly planning a 150MW data center campus in Lelystad, Netherlands, with an investment of €1.5 billion ($1.7bn). The project will comprise three two-story buildings at the Flevokust Haven industrial estate, powered directly by the Maxima gas-fired power plant. Construction is expected to begin next year, with phased completion through 2028 or 2029.

feed.title: Charles River Extends Commitment to Advanced Therapy Developers with Second Incubator Program Cohort
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories announced the second cohort of its Cell & Gene Therapy Incubator Program (CIP), supporting early-stage biotechnology with scientific expertise and development capabilities. The program features four advanced therapy developers and two enabling technology developers, aiming to accelerate the creation of life-changing therapies. Participants receive mentorship, regulatory guidance, and access to Charles River’s global network to advance their programs from concept to cure.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Predict a 25.78% Upside in Omnicom (OMC): Here's What You Should Know
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts anticipate a significant upside for Omnicom (OMC), with a mean price target of $97.38 suggesting a 25.8% gain from its current $77.42. This optimism is supported by a strong agreement among analysts and a positive trend in earnings estimate revisions, leading to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) for the stock. However, the article cautions investors to treat price targets with skepticism, as business incentives can sometimes lead to inflated projections.

feed.title: McDonald’s pulls Grinchy AI-made Christmas ad after avalanche of complaints
feed.summary: McDonald's Netherlands has removed an AI-generated Christmas advertisement after receiving widespread criticism for its "Grinchy" and un-festive tone. The ad, which depicted holiday misfortunes and suggested seeking refuge at McDonald's, was viewed millions of times through reposts despite being pulled from official channels. While the ad was created using AI, a 10-person crew spent five weeks working on it, highlighting the effort involved in AI-assisted creative projects.

feed.title: Why the Marzetti Company’s master data project is paying off in the AI era
feed.summary: The Marzetti Company's proactive master data consolidation project, completed before the AI boom, has strategically positioned them ahead of competitors. CIO Liam Durbin highlights how clean, unified data is crucial for leveraging AI effectively, enabling initiatives like accounts payable automation. Durbin advises other companies to fully fund and staff master data projects, despite their perceived lack of glamor, to ensure robust data quality essential for AI and ERP success.
